deconstruction_targets: Witches who offer children to devils
Witches who bewitch judges and manipulate legal proceedings
Witches who use religious symbols (Crucifix) for evil charms
Witches who blaspheme against the Virgin Mary and Jesus Christ
Henchmen and knights who seduce women and cause their deaths through witchcraft
Neighbors who accuse others of witchcraft to cover up adultery or personal vendettas

scapegoat.name: Witches
attributes: cause illness and death through witchcraft
kill children or offer them to devils
scapegoat.justification: The text consistently portrays witches as the primary source of moral, physical, and spiritual corruption, attributing all societal ills — from individual illness to judicial obstruction — directly to their actions. Their abjuration of faith is cited as the gravest crime, justifying their condemnation and eradication.

moral_justification: The text frames witchcraft as a direct assault on God's creation, the Catholic Faith, and social order. It portrays witches as agents of the devil who corrupt individuals through blasphemy, murder, and betrayal, thereby deserving divine and earthly punishment to restore moral and spiritual integrity.
summary: This passage is a highly charged deconstruction of witchcraft, detailing its alleged crimes against people, religion, and justice. It uses vivid, emotionally intense language to condemn witches as the root cause of personal misfortune, judicial corruption, and religious blasphemy, while praising the Archduke's efforts to eradicate them. No constructive solutions are offered beyond eradication.
